programmes.show_description: The station is prepared for a visit by the chairman of British Railways, but it turns out to be a different Doctor Beeching altogether. S2 E8: Action Stations
Alone in wartime France, Gary has to deal with a nasty Nazi, a friendly resistance fighter, and the fact that his double has plans to take his place in London. S5 E7: But We Think You Have to Go
Mr. Bean turns the most ordinary situations into moments of excruciating embarrassment as he visits a department store and gets carried away. E2: The Return of Mr. Bean
When DC Chris Skelton opens CID's post and discovers a human hand, DCI Gene Hunt and his team find that they have a murder to solve. S3 E2
Sandra visits the doctor longing for news of a happy event, and it dawns on Mrs Hutchinson that she will become a granny at some point... which she is not thrilled about. S9 E3: Various Kinds of Old
A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!
Malcolm decides to move out when his mother interferes yet again, and David offers information about the enterprise allowance scheme. S2 E4: Visiting II
A surprise visitor in the form of attractive Emma turns up at Tracey's house - Tracey is welcoming, Dorien is shocked to the core, and Travis is over the moon. S12 E6: Abandoned Love
Mr. Bean wreaks havoc at Harrods when he goes Christmas shopping, then has trouble stuffing a turkey on Christmas Day. E7: Merry Christmas, Mr. Bean
Several months have passed - Amy is working, but David is still unemployed and lies to her that his doctor has told him he will have a nervous breakdown unless he takes a holiday. S3 E1: Winter Break

Rigsby is soon calling the Samaritans to stop a suicide attempt when a new lodger arrives at the house, but which of the residents will come off worse? S3 E4: The Good Samaritans
Wolfie and Ken are evicted from their dingy hovel - there's a self-contained flat at Shirley's that is empty, but she'll have to get the idea past her dad first. S1 E3: Abide With Me
A photographer takes advantage of Aveline, while Jack dabbles in art. S1 E4
Sandra is home alone while Derek is at work, so they agree to let the spare room as company for Sandra. Who is Dawn and what is her link to Carol? S9 E1: There's No Place Like Away from Home
It's Jack and May's wedding anniversary, but May is worried that she might be pregnant - meanwhile, Arnold fears that he is about to get the sack. S2 E9: A Pregnant Pause
Cissy goes to the polls at the local election, James gets a job offer from Sir Ralph, and Teddy goes too far but Alf suggests a plan. S3 E6 Night of Reckoning
Robin has taken his new girlfriend Angie out on a couple of dates, and now he hopes to bring her back to the flat to have his wicked way with her. S3 E2: Come Into My Parlour
At the start of a new school year, Mr. Brown has two new students... as well as ten old ones who did not pass their exam. S2 E1: All Present If Not Correct
Alf's eyesight is worsening, which is fine until he accidentally enters the Ladies' toilet and is arrested as a sex pest. S1 E2
Sandra is home alone while Derek is at work, so they agree to let the spare room as company for Sandra. Who is Dawn and what is her link to Carol? S9 E1: There's No Place Like Away from Home
A photographer takes advantage of Aveline, while Jack dabbles in art. S1 E4
Arthur plans to boost the membership of the Winchester Club, but ends up in trouble over a stolen karaoke machine. S8 E9: The Greatest Show in Willesden
It's Jack and May's wedding anniversary, but May is worried that she might be pregnant - meanwhile, Arnold fears that he is about to get the sack. S2 E9: A Pregnant Pause
Phoebe's in the present, Yvonne is in the past, and when they meet in the middle, the sparks really begin to fly. S5 E8: Have You Ever Seen a Dream Walking
Mr. Bean goes to a swimming pool but struggles with the diving board, attempts to leave a car park in style and demonstrates some sandwich-making. E3: The Curse of Mr. Bean
It's the run-up to the 1983 General Election, and pressure mounts on DCI Gene Hunt and his team as they investigate a series of politically-motivated arson attacks. S3 E3
The Boswell family face eviction from their house as the council redevelops the local area, and Carol has a new boyfriend and really likes him... but why has he gone cool on her? S9 E4: Weeds
A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!
Peggy petitions against the demolition of a disused church, Malcolm has problems at work when receipts go missing, and Brenda has a go at Pamela for trying to set her up with Terry. S2 E5: Missing
When Travis has serious school news that he's scared to break to his mum, Sharon decides she'll pass herself off as Tracey and sort out his problems. S12 E7: Blonde on Blonde
More hilarious mishaps with the bumbling Mr. Bean, this time as he books into a hotel room and makes himself well and truly at home but locks himself out. E8: Mr. Bean in Room 426
David is caught out when Amy tells him that Tommy Wright is dead, so he and Linda adopt disguises to avoid each other's spouses as they plan to move to another hotel. S3 E2: Deja Vu

When Miss Jones raises suspicions about new tenant Madeline, Rigsby is not too bothered about her brand of 'modern interpretive dancing' but isn't that keen on her partner. S3 E5: Fawcett's Python
Ken falls in love with the girlfriend of one of Wolfie's prison-bound friends and arranges to take her away for the weekend, but Wolfie tags along to keep an eye on things. S1 E4: The Weekend
The Boswells decide upon a burial at sea when Freddie's seafaring brother dies. S1 E5
Sandra still isn't pregnant and she craves attention from Derek; in the meantime, Carol's cousin Willy comes stay and only gets on with Derek. Could this help Sandra? S9 E2: The Sixth Day
The staff discover that the station is a hundred years old and decide to have a street party to celebrate. S2 E10: Ton Up
Lord Meldrum decides to treat the whole household to a picnic at Peabody Hall, but the family sense Lady Agatha has a hand in it. S3 E7 A Day in the Country
There is a formal dinner dance at the firm where Jo and Chrissy work, but Chrissy's dance partner has injured himself and so Robin becomes her partner instead. S3 E3: I Won't Dance, Don't Ask Me
The class becomes excited when Miss Courtney announces that Queen Elizabeth II will be paying a visit to their school. S2 E2: Queen for a Day
Alf feels that he and Else are entitled to home help. However, it seems that the perfect person for the job is a very flamboyant gay black man. S1 E3

Arthur enters the food business, but when the chef he employs goes on the run from a group of gangsters, Arthur is left to cater for a policeman's retirement party. S8 E10: Too Many Crooks
